contracts.shape:
The sole normative protocol pin is Baton v0.15.1 at upstream commit 3fb4d275ae8a151f6287e7b9279d71628b12eea0, source archive sha256:8f0839ea897374eb10d6db2a789939714727739621babef1117d74cbf4488d2f, and upstream root VERSION blob 5f1dd0af59642311ee04e018a0023562d4dde008 whose exact bytes are v0.15.1 plus LF. Sworn's internal/adopt/baton/VERSION is a separate multi-line adopting-repository manifest whose committed blob and parsed tag/SHA/digest must agree across participating refs and with this pin. Normative vendored JSON bytes are byte-identical to that tag. The complete deterministic offline installer-input tar is SHA-256 27d5021cb3ec258a7fd7a5feb6eed92968be0e6cb439e2951da7c6b368e0ca15 and Git blob 39ae650dfe0282b0fa8bda14e1a01e7084077702; exact extracted upstream installers define the canonical Codex and Claude managed trees under umask 0022. doctor --sync-baton repairs three disjoint managed roots and Sworn VERSION sentinels in one rollback-protected transaction whose complete recovery authority is durable before the first replacement, and recovery blocks later installation until exact pre-run restoration. Clean-CI built-binary parity uses the committed test-only Git bundle internal/baton/testdata/fixture/baton-v0.15.1.bundle, whose canonical bytes concatenate ASCII `# v2 git bundle
3ba5f70435ff1ef3ea819def7b06c126fdb269d8 refs/tags/v0.15.1

` and Git 2.43.0 stdout from `printf 'refs/tags/v0.15.1
' | git pack-objects --stdout --revs --window=0 --depth=0 --compression=9 --threads=1 --no-reuse-delta --no-reuse-object --no-sparse --delta-base-offset`, with SHA-256 cba3796ed382623f35abc568183e3a5a0d4a82335cebd4589989d0ae41b43ad5, Git blob 77e5b4cc7210a41ce8779bc352a1f487101fb80e, size 2505826 bytes, annotated tag object 3ba5f70435ff1ef3ea819def7b06c126fdb269d8, and peeled commit 3fb4d275ae8a151f6287e7b9279d71628b12eea0. The committed bytes and pinned identities, not regeneration under an arbitrary Git version, are normative. It is test authority only and never a runtime dependency. contracts.json.owner is the sole active C-01 ownership authority: S02's frozen statement that it owns C-01 is historical narrative superseded by this re-plan and can never satisfy current or cutover authority.
contracts.rationale: Every later record and success path must consume one immutable protocol rather than reinterpret issue history or the running environment; S20 owns the active pin after S02's failed boundary is retired through S19. The explicit owner field takes precedence over frozen historical slice prose.

contracts.shape: spec.references is the only normative discovery surface and follows planning/normative-clarifications.md section 3. Duplicate-safe spec validation, physical workspace-root discovery, canonical source-path agreement, lexical-path validation, and physical confinement run in that exact hard-failure order before dispatch. Safe missing, non-regular, unreadable, invalid UTF-8/JSON/schema, record-release, contract-count, or sibling-ID failures render the exact ordered UNRESOLVED vocabulary for the ambiguity model. Resolved UTF-8 artifacts are path-sorted, deduplicated and verbatim; resolution is one level deep with no network. The dedicated spec-ambiguity report remains distinct from generic llm-check-report-v1.
contracts.rationale: Planning and migration are only trustworthy when every normative input is explicit, confined and deterministically resolvable.

contracts.shape: A strict record_version=1 JSON record pins release, protocol name, exact version, upstream SHA, sha256 source digest, upstream root VERSION blob OID, origin native or migrated, and authority planning or current, with the exact conditional migration_receipt_path from planning/normative-clarifications.md section 4. Every registered CLI spelling/alias and MCP tool has exactly one operation-level policy there. Current operations require one committed current marker blob byte-identical on every derived participating authority ref and exactly equal to the embedded binary pin; separately, each ref's committed internal/adopt/baton/VERSION manifest blob must be equal across participants and its parsed tag/SHA/digest must equal the marker and binary. Historical inspection selects the oldest commit in the newest uninterrupted first-parent suffix where the requested path has its current blob and returns that tree's actual adopting-repository manifest path/blob/parsed pin. Missing, dirty-only, shallow, divergent, malformed, unsupported or binary-mismatched evidence fails before side effects; C-13 is the sole native planning-to-current edge and C-12 owns the distinct receipt-bound migrated edge.
contracts.rationale: Live mutation authority and truthful historical inspection are separate facts and must both come from committed evidence.

contracts.shape: From immutable start, review and sync commits, the operation emits the exact authored candidate path set, each canonical excluded path once, Baton's byte-exact header-plus-path mode-and-blob manifest and sha256 input fingerprint, immutable semantic identity, a separately moving freshness frontier, and Baton's exact normalized prompt-diff invocation using planning/normative-clarifications.md sections 5 and 6. It requires a completely clean index/worktree, uses the exact release-root predicate, eleven lockfile basenames and recognized synchronization predicate, and never reads worktree bytes as semantic authority. While authority is planning only, the schema-, claim-schema-, receipt-schema-, and manifest-digest-pinned sealed v2 authority recognizes only four ordered historical merges: exact S01 live-review envelopes, exact S02 non-dispatch historical-report envelopes at 60097cfa plus live-review envelopes at 2a17443, eight release-record path tuples, and the single independently replayed 7696c9bf noncanonical index projection whose actual bytes never become current authority. The same authority declares one exact physically dispatched and historically ledgered but invalid/non-authoritative orphan PASS, its lifecycle/hold prefix correction, and one fresh manual-adapter replacement closure bound to hold 3ac42c01 whose claim commit consumes the sole slot and issues a non-resumable permit. The claim stores only a random continuity-token hash; a valid result requires a receipt revealing the preimage and binding the claim commit, invocation, report blob and verdict. No orphan response, report, verdict, fingerprint or lifecycle fact is reusable. Every topology, structural ancestry, non-record/shared path, unlisted release-record path and all other deterministic index checks remain ordinary. Report reuse compares immutable semantic identity only; changed track or release-wt heads trigger frontier revalidation rather than identity drift.
contracts.rationale: Every report, freshness decision and rollback must identify the same semantic candidate without adapter-local path heuristics.

contracts.shape: Each compact status entry remains exact Baton shape and pins role, phase, cycle, invocation ID, release-confined full-report path, committed report blob OID, review-scope head, sha256 input fingerprint, PASS or FAIL, and exact blocking finding IDs. The full report validates first against exact llm-check-report-v1 and then planning/sworn-maintainability-report-overlay-v1.schema.json: each blocker has a structured remediate_in_scope or re_slice action plus C-02-valid sorted unique non-empty required_touchpoints, and an engine-owned top-level sworn object persists immutable scope provenance plus the freshness frontier outside closed Baton fields. Duplicate keys and rewritten, missing or deleted blobs fail closed; only exact committed immutable semantic and lifecycle identity may be reused after frontier revalidation and without dispatch.
contracts.rationale: The compact ledger is authority only when it is cryptographically tied to the exact committed full report and semantic input.

contracts.shape: Committed first-parent history enforces the complete transition, citation, and caller-authority table in planning/normative-clarifications.md section 7: one preflight, optional closure only after preflight FAIL, one fresh authoritative Verifier only after Implementer PASS, cycle 0 then at most one Coach-approved cycle 1 resume, exact authoritative dispatch budgets, immutable start, append-only authoritative report prefixes, Coach-supplied decision/rationale/identity and inclusive permitted touchpoints, evidence-derived citation identities and blocking-ID union, command-captured immutable timestamp, and implementation_head null on terminal failure. Under planning authority only, the sealed v2 declaration recognizes the exact non-authoritative f4ef orphan/lifecycle/hold sequence as an invalid pre-C06 attempt plus one prefix-restoration correction; that orphan consumes no C-06 slot. The separately ratified replacement claim commit consumes the sole exceptional dispatch and issues one non-resumable permit; only a token-matching continuity receipt plus valid report consumes the ordinary cycle-0 closure row. No other prefix removal, reset, repeat or special budget is legal. Cycle-1 authored paths must stay within immutable permitted touchpoints; structured re_slice or boundary expansion terminates before remediation or further dispatch. No adapter, flag or displayed state may waive, reset, reorder, infer, or synthesize lifecycle authority.
contracts.rationale: Role authority is a committed state machine, not a convention reconstructed independently by each public command.

contracts.shape: Only exact paths declared in board.shared_touchpoints may receive contributions from multiple tracks, every contributing track and unique non-empty region must be declared, and the result is Git's canonical committed-blob three-way composition. Merge drivers, attributes, filters, worktree bytes, manual edits and custom conflict resolution cannot influence the result; malformed declarations, non-blob entries, conflicts or tree mismatch block.
contracts.rationale: Parallel tracks are safe only when shared-file composition is declared, deterministic and independent of local Git behavior.

contracts.shape: For each path, active authoritative ownership intervals and rollback-backed retired intervals are derived from committed track ancestry, authoritative review heads, and the exact synchronization and release-record composition decision table in planning/normative-clarifications.md sections 6 and 8, including only a digest-pinned planning-authority live_review reconciliation whose authorization exactly matches the consuming slice, immutable start, exact review head, start-exclusive/head-inclusive first-parent interval, and freshness_revalidation purpose. historical_report_revalidation belongs to C-04 report reconstruction and C-13 cutover, not C-08 freshness; the orphan, hold correction and replacement claim likewise grant no ownership or freshness authority. A recognized intersecting post-frontier sync disjoint from later authority returns one deterministic local invalidation plan that preserves ledger/adjudication, clears implementation_head, records the exact invalidation tuple, and transitions to failed_verification/re_slice_required; S15 alone may commit it locally with no push or release-wt merge. Later overlap, unrecognized syncs, stale frontiers, shipped authority, an unavailable or mismatched historical entry or authorization, or historical ownership presented as current PASS evidence block without mutation.
contracts.rationale: Freshness must preserve unrelated verified work while refusing to bless bytes that no current authoritative review covers.

contracts.shape: One terminal predicate validates exact protocol authority, schema and record identity, lifecycle terminal state, report blob identity, semantic scope, evidence freshness, shared composition, rollback equality under planning/normative-clarifications.md section 8, and deferral legitimacy. It is not a precondition for active maintainability review or adjudication, which validate C-03 through C-08 plus operation-specific C-06 eligibility; S13 first consumes C-09 only at cutover. An ordinary rollback envelope is every authored path from the failed slice start through the verified rollback slice implementation_head, including gaps, rollback-authored, generated, and lock paths; exclude only the release root and recognized merge-only contributions, never subtract later authority, and require every tuple or absence to equal the original start tree. Post-sync rollback separately uses invalidated_review_head, exact parent-2 baseline, and later-authority disjointness. A valid unstarted deferral and rollback-backed terminal re-slice deferral are distinct; raw deferred is never sufficient. Every normal and idempotent merge, release and shipping path reruns the predicate.
contracts.rationale: All adapters need one conservative answer to whether committed evidence authorizes integration.

contracts.shape: sworn maintainability review accepts only Implementer preflight or closure and Verifier authoritative role-phase pairs; sworn maintainability adjudicate accepts only resume_in_scope or re_slice through one typed request. Coach supplies decision, non-empty rationale and approved_by plus resume-only permitted touchpoints; committed evidence derives citation identities and blocking IDs, and the command captures approved_at once. Both commands require a clean start, atomically write coherent report, compact status and journal transitions, commit and push the exact track ref before protocol success, and durably persist valid FAIL. Recovery follows planning/normative-clarifications.md section 10: uncommitted partial evidence fails unchanged with zero dispatch; only an exact coherent commit may resume its missing push with zero dispatch. No inferred authority, dry-run, no-push, force, waiver or lifecycle override exists.
contracts.rationale: The public operation that owns a lifecycle transition must also own its durable restart boundary.

contracts.shape: sworn ship is a read-only pre-cutover readiness gate and never records deployment. sworn mark-shipped is the native adapter for exact Baton v0.15.1 /mark-shipped: from the clean primary integration worktree it fetches origin and blocks unless origin is an ancestor/equal, revalidates complete terminal authority, and returns the exact no-deployment-evidence no-op when no verified slice remains. Otherwise it requires a deployed commit, checks containment when the conventional release-merge identity exists or records Baton's exact legacy skip when it does not, moves only verified slices to shipped, sets last_updated_by/at, adds the exact ship block {shipped_at,deployed_commit,deploy_ref,shipped_by}, preserves the pure-plan board byte-identically, re-renders slice/aggregate/recent-activity views from board plus statuses, and creates one local status/index commit. It never pushes, merges, builds, deploys or cleans and does not re-handoff existing shipped records.
contracts.rationale: Readiness to deploy and truthful post-deployment state are distinct auditable facts.

contracts.shape: A human-ratified Planner delta validates exactly against planning/plan-delta-v1.schema.json and fixes source objects, owner seeds, byte-exact mutations, one-to-one rollback links, each target's separate local and remote compare-and-swap heads, and an immutable non-colliding receipt path. Its numeric RFC3339 offset is preserved; mutation paths cannot enter plan-deltas; each original and rollback slice ID is unique; receipt_path differs from every mutation and canonical delta path. A target's heads must match, except that an exact C-08/S15 local-only invalidation permits the local head to be that validated one-parent commit and the remote head to be its parent; propagation preserves that invalidation as parent 1 while advancing both refs from their declared heads. Its canonical plan ref contains the delta without embedding its own commit OID and that Planner-only path never enters the release transaction. The deterministic receipt validates against planning/protocol-migration-receipt-v1.schema.json and derives receipt_id, planner authority and created_at exactly. Every unsigned canonical commit object has exact tree, ordered parents, fixed identity, Unix-seconds plus preserved numeric offset, no extra headers/signature, and one LF-terminated subject; not_started, locally_committed, fully_propagated and migration-only fully_activated are reconstructed from those bytes. In-place migration is pristine-only and planning/local-first-migration-manifest.json is a complete 17-mutation delta including downstream contracts repair and golden SHA-1 Planner/receipt/transaction/activation identities; migrate writes planning authority and activate performs the separate gate without fabricated evidence.
contracts.rationale: Planner judgment remains human-led while record mutation and propagation become deterministic and restart-safe.

contracts.shape: After S01 and S03-S13 plus S19-s02-v015-rollback and S20-v015-parity-portable-fixture are freshly verified, and original S02 is a C-09-valid rollback-backed terminal deferral, and T1 then T2 are canonically integrated by the pinned Baton v0.15.1 Track Integrator role under manual planning authority, sworn maintainability cutover <release> is the sole one-way native activation operation. It recognizes and revalidates both exact two-parent bootstrap integration commits and independently reconstructs the sealed v2 schema/manifest/claim-schema/receipt-schema digests, four merge/parent/base/path tuples, every exact live and historical-report envelope, the 7696c9bf actual-versus-expected index projection, the authoritative S01/S02 historical report ledger, S02's immutable rollback link and exact S19 tree equality, and S20's fresh active report ledger, plus the permanently non-authoritative orphan PASS plus invalid lifecycle/hold correction, and the single replacement claim/token receipt/report disposition. Declaration, claim, receipt, filesystem and ledger consumption sets must be exactly equal; the stale index and orphan never satisfy current or cutover authority. It runs on clean local/remote-equal release-wt before T5/T6 refs exist, revalidates the exact pin plus all planning, ambiguity, engine and evidence gates, changes only protocol.json authority planning to current, commits and pushes once, retires all v2 recognition and bootstrap-closure replacement authority, and follows the committed-only recovery table in planning/normative-clarifications.md sections 4 and 10. No caller-selected ref, force, downgrade, waiver, historical-entry extension, orphan promotion, second claim, second bootstrap closure dispatch, inherited S02 current authority, rollback bypass, or alternate auto-promotion exists.
contracts.rationale: The self-hosting release needs one explicit, auditable boundary between manual v0.15 bootstrap evidence and automated current authority.

contracts.shape: Only when the structured input is exactly Baton llm-check-report-v1 at $id https://baton.sawy3r.net/schemas/llm-check-report-v1.json with SHA-256 ed38b77823af1b329c1dc7d8427b08849f15690d5afa9625e196505bdfa5b65b may openai/ Responses text.format or openai-completions/ response_format send the deterministic strict schema named llm-check-report-v1-openai-envelope. It requires check, verdict, findings and each canonical required finding field id, severity, blocking, title, detail; seals every emitted object; and contains no allOf, if, then, else, or not. It is an outbound transport envelope only: the canonical source bytes, local llm-check-report-v1 validation, and S04 requested/emitted check equality remain authoritative after emission. Exact spec-ambiguity-report-v1, digest mismatch, and unknown generic-report-family identities reject before HTTP with no schema mutation, synthetic report, map/array reconstruction, retry, raw Verify, or text fallback. xAI, tool-call, and unprofiled/non-OpenAI paths retain their existing supplied-schema behavior.
contracts.rationale: The consumer needs a real accepted ac-satisfaction emission only after the transport constraint is proven without weakening or re-owning S04 semantics.

contracts.shape: Only a direct `openrouter/` model route may advertise structured output, and it does so solely through the existing OpenAI-compatible forced function named `emit_structured_output`. The exact canonical schema supplied to ChatStructuredJSON becomes that function's parameters without strict projection, OpenAI envelope compilation, source mutation, raw-text fallback, retry, or synthesis. The function choice is forced and a missing/invalid tool call fails locally. `SWORN_OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` is valid only on the direct client path and exists to direct the public command to a local test endpoint; ordinary direct routing uses https://openrouter.ai/api/v1. A Sworn hosted-proxy route for an `openrouter/` model, Ollama, and every other unprofiled provider remain structured-output unsupported. The release proof pins `SWORN_DIRECT=1` and model `openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.2`, but that customer-selected proof input does not establish a Sworn default. After emission, the unchanged canonical Baton validation and S04 requested/emitted check equality are the sole semantic authority.
contracts.rationale: The selected provider path needs deterministic transport evidence while preserving the default-deny trust boundary for every other direct and proxied route.
